title: Add Memory Skill Bundling Regression Coverage
description: Extend automated coverage around isolated execution root preparation so the new `memory` skill presence is asserted where appropriate, preventing regressions in allowlist wiring or skill copying.
acceptance_criteria: Relevant tests assert that supported agent types include `memory` in their loaded skill set or copied skill directories.
Coverage exercises the isolated execution root path rather than only checking static constants.
Test updates remain focused on skill wiring and do not broaden into unrelated refactors.

handoff_summary.completed: Added TestMemorySkillInAllowlist with test_all_worker_types_have_memory (subTest loop over developer/tester/planner/documentation/review) and test_scheduler_does_not_have_memory. Added TestMemorySkillCopiedToExecRoot with 4 tests exercising the isolated execution root path: memory skill directory present for developer, memory skill directory present for tester, loaded_skills metadata includes memory for developer, loaded_skills metadata includes memory for tester.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Tests are purely additive and scoped to skill wiring. They rely on .agents/skills/memory existing in the repo (confirmed present).
handoff_summary.next_action: Hand off to tester agent to run the full test suite and confirm new tests pass.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None — changes are confined to tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py with no overlap with sibling beads.

conflict_risks: None — changes are confined to tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py with no overlap with sibling beads.

execution_history.summary: Added memory skill regression coverage to tests/test_config_wiring_phase3.py. Two new test classes: TestMemorySkillInAllowlist (static allowlist checks for all 5 worker agent types) and TestMemorySkillCopiedToExecRoot (exercises prepare_isolated_execution_root and asserts memory skill directory and metadata are present for developer and tester agents).

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: `uv run python -c "import codex_orchestrator"` or `uv run python -m py_compile <file>`.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not `unittest discover`, not `unittest tests.<module>`, not any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
